steps.observed: Directory created, no pre-existing auth files

steps.action: Insert test bearer token into local worker database
steps.expected: Token hash stored in user_tokens table linked to user
steps.observed: Token inserted for user ia-r3-cli-fbcb@example.com (tier: pro)

steps.action: Run spikes login --token <bearer_token> with isolated HOME
steps.expected: Token stored in auth.toml, success message displayed
steps.observed: Command succeeded with '🗡️  Logged in successfully' message
File created at /tmp/spikes-utv-ia-r3-cli-fbcb-home/Library/Application Support/spikes/auth.toml with -rw------- (0600) permissions

steps.expected: TOML format with [auth] section and token field

steps.action: Verify /me endpoint works with test token via curl
steps.expected: JSON response with email and tier fields

steps.action: Run whoami with stored token using SPIKES_API_URL pointing to local worker
steps.observed: SPIKES_API_URL=http://localhost:8787 spikes whoami displayed: Email: ia-r3-cli-fbcb@example.com, Tier: pro

steps.expected: Machine-readable JSON output with email and tier
Error message: 'Not logged in. Run 'spikes login' first.'
steps.observed: Error: IO error: Not logged in. Run 'spikes login' first.

outputs: Error: IO error: Not logged in. Run 'spikes login' first.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-AUTH-014, VAL-AUTH-016). VAL-AUTH-014 confirms spikes login --token correctly stores bearer token in platform-appropriate config directory (~/Library/Application Support/spikes/auth.toml on macOS) with 0600 permissions. VAL-AUTH-016 confirms spikes whoami correctly displays email and tier when authenticated, and shows appropriate error when unauthenticated. Using SPIKES_API_URL=http://localhost:8787 allowed testing against local worker which returns proper JSON from /me endpoint, enabling full end-to-end validation.
